meta.title: JID — Employment & mentorship platform
meta.description: Connecting talent with opportunities — transparent jobs, mentorship, and trusted profiles.

hero.title: A job market that deserves transparency — for every applicant and every employer
hero.subtitle: JID brings job search, mentorship, and company profiles together — Arabic-first with clear communication standards.
hero.body: No empty promises. No unsourced statistics. Just real tools to see where your application stands and who can guide your career.

problem.title: A real problem that does not need fabricated numbers
problem.subtitle: Recurring experiences in Saudi hiring — addressed with practical transparency, not marketing stats.

applicants.headline: The vast majority of applicants
applicants.body: never receive a clear update on their application outcome

waiting.headline: Long waiting periods
waiting.body: with no clear deadline for a response

graduates.headline: A wide segment of graduates
graduates.body: do not know their application status for weeks

modules.title: Platform modules
modules.subtitle: Eight connected paths — from discovering roles to tracking applications and mentorship.

catalog.description: Discover employers and their public profiles before you apply.

pdpl.title: Your privacy under Saudi PDPL principles

vision2030.title: Empowering talent and a more transparent labour market
vision2030.body: JID supports a clearer path to opportunity and stronger career journeys — in line with Vision 2030 goals to empower citizens and diversify the economy. This text expresses the platform direction; it is not a verbatim quote from Vision 2030 documents.
vision2030.attributionNote: Platform direction is inspired by Vision 2030 goals — not a direct quotation.

entity.title: For employers & universities
entity.body: You cannot become an admin by signing up directly — search the catalog and claim your profile.

meta.title: About JID
meta.description: Learn about JID's mission and values — an Arabic-first employment and mentorship platform.

about.title: About {name}
about.intro: JID is a Saudi platform connecting job seekers, graduates, mentors, and employers — making applications, responses, and guidance clearer for everyone.

mission.title: Our mission
mission.body: We build practical tools that reduce hiring opacity: a unified profile, job board, application radar, and mentorship — without unsourced marketing claims.

values.title: Our values
values.subtitle: Principles that guide every feature we ship.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.description: Clear application states, published deadlines, and honest communication about what the platform can and cannot do.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description: Data minimisation, access controls, and respect for data-subject rights under Saudi PDPL.

empowerment.title: Empowerment
empowerment.description: Tools that help candidates present their skills, mentors contribute, and organisations manage their presence with professional dignity.

trust.title: Trust
trust.description: Verified profiles through claim review and commitment signals — without unverified statistic wars.

meta.title: Personal Data Protection — JID
meta.description: Your rights under Saudi PDPL and how JID handles your personal data.

pdplPage.title: Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL)
pdplPage.intro: This page explains how JID handles personal data and data-subject rights on the platform.
pdplPage.legalBasis: JID practices are designed to align with the Personal Data Protection Law issued by Royal Decree No. (M/19) dated 9/2/1443H.
pdplPage.legalNote: This notice is informational and is not a formal compliance certificate. For binding legal advice, consult qualified counsel or SDAIA.

scope.title: Scope
scope.body: The law applies to processing of personal data relating to individuals in the Kingdom. When you use JID, we may process account, profile, application, and communication data — for the purpose stated for each service.

rights.title: Data-subject rights
rights.subtitle: Six fundamental rights under the law, supported by platform processes where applicable:

informed.title: Right to be informed
informed.description: Know why your data is collected and the legal basis for processing before or at collection.

access.title: Right of access
access.description: Request access to your personal data held by us within statutory limits.

rectification.title: Right to rectification
rectification.description: Request correction of inaccurate data or completion via account settings or by contacting us.

erasure.title: Right to erasure
erasure.description: Request deletion when a legal ground exists, subject to remaining legal or contractual obligations.

withdrawConsent.title: Right to withdraw consent
withdrawConsent.description: Withdraw consent for consent-based processing where available, without affecting prior lawful processing.

complaint.title: Right to lodge a complaint
complaint.description: File a complaint with the competent authority — the Saudi Data & AI Authority (SDAIA) — if you believe a violation occurred.

items.minimization: Collect only data necessary to deliver the service.
items.retention: Retain data only as long as needed, then delete or anonymise where applicable.
items.breach: Procedures to address data breaches per regulatory requirements when they occur.

meta.title: Privacy Policy — JID
meta.description: How JID collects, uses, stores, and protects your personal data.

privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.summary: This policy describes what personal data JID processes when you use our employment and mentorship platform, why we process it, who we share it with, and the rights available to you under Saudi law.

intro.p1: JID (جِد) operates an Arabic-first platform connecting job seekers, graduates, mentors, universities, and employers in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. Protecting your privacy is central to that mission.
intro.p2: This Privacy Policy applies to visitors, registered users, entity administrators, mentors, and anyone who contacts us through the platform. By creating an account or continuing to use JID, you acknowledge that you have read this policy.

collect.title: Data we collect
collect.intro: We collect only data that is reasonably necessary to provide the service. Depending on how you use JID, this may include:

account.label: Account & identity data
account.body: Name, email address, phone number (when verified), password hash, role, language preference, and authentication session metadata.

profile.label: Profile & career data
profile.body: Headline, biography, skills, education, target sectors, CV content, visibility settings, avatar, and smart links you choose to publish.

applications.label: Applications & job activity
applications.body: Saved and declared applications, application status, self-declaration timestamps, verified application emails, and interaction with job listings.

mentorship.label: Mentorship data
mentorship.body: Mentor application details, expertise areas, meeting schedules, feedback, and messages exchanged through mentorship features.

entity.label: Entity & claim data
entity.body: Company or university profile information, claim requests, verification documents submitted for review, and staff audit actions related to your organisation.

technical.label: Technical & usage data
technical.body: IP address, browser type, device information, pages viewed, feature-flag evaluations, and aggregated analytics events (where enabled and consented).

communications.label: Communications
communications.body: Support messages, contact form submissions, notification preferences, and email delivery status received from our email provider.

use.title: How we use your data
use.intro: We process personal data for the following purposes:

items.service: Providing core platform features: accounts, profiles, job board, application radar, mentorship, and entity catalogues.
items.matching: Connecting candidates with opportunities and mentors, including visibility rules you control in settings.
items.notifications: Sending transactional emails and in-app notifications about applications, claims, meetings, and security events.
items.security: Detecting abuse, enforcing role-based access, preventing fraud, and maintaining audit trails for staff actions.
items.analytics: Measuring product usage in aggregate to improve performance and accessibility, subject to your cookie choices where applicable.
items.legal: Complying with applicable law, responding to lawful requests, and establishing or defending legal claims.

use.legalBasis: Where Saudi PDPL requires a legal basis, we rely on contract performance (to deliver the service you requested), legitimate interests (security and product improvement, balanced against your rights), consent (where we ask explicitly, e.g. optional analytics cookies), and legal obligation where applicable.

sharing.title: Third-party sharing
sharing.intro: We do not sell your personal data. We share data only with processors that help us operate JID, under contractual safeguards and the minimum necessary scope:

supabase.title: Supabase (hosting & database)
supabase.body: JID is hosted on Supabase cloud infrastructure. Account credentials, profiles, applications, and most platform data are stored in Supabase-managed PostgreSQL databases with Row Level Security policies. Supabase processes data on our instructions as a data processor. Their servers may be located outside the Kingdom; we assess transfers and apply appropriate safeguards as required by applicable law.

resend.title: Resend (transactional email)
resend.body: We use Resend to deliver transactional emails such as verification codes, claim decisions, staff invitations, and notification digests. Resend receives recipient email addresses, message subject lines, and delivery metadata. We do not use Resend to send unsolicited marketing without your consent.

other.title: Other recipients
other.body: Approved employers may see candidate data you expose through your profile visibility and application choices. Universities may see aggregated student statistics where you opt in. We may also disclose data to competent authorities when required by law, or to professional advisers bound by confidentiality.

sharing.noSale: We will not rent or sell personal data to data brokers or advertising networks.

rights.title: Your rights under PDPL
rights.body: As a data subject in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, you have rights under the Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL), including access, rectification, erasure, and complaint rights. We summarise these rights and how to exercise them on our dedicated <pdplLink>PDPL notice</pdplLink> page.

items.access: Request access to personal data we hold about you, subject to identity verification and legal exceptions.
items.rectification: Update inaccurate data through account settings or by contacting us.
items.erasure: Request deletion when there is no overriding legal or contractual reason to retain the data.
items.portability: Request a copy of your data in a structured, commonly used format where technically feasible.
items.withdraw: Withdraw consent for consent-based processing without affecting prior lawful processing.
items.complaint: Lodge a complaint with the Saudi Data & AI Authority (SDAIA) if you believe your rights have been violated.

retention.title: Retention periods
retention.intro: We retain personal data only for as long as necessary for the purposes described in this policy, unless a longer period is required by law:

account.label: Active accounts
account.body: Profile and account data are retained while your account is active and for up to 24 months after closure to handle disputes, legal holds, and backup rotation.

applications.label: Applications & declarations
applications.body: Application records and self-declaration history are retained for up to 36 months after the related job closes or the application reaches a terminal status, unless you or the employer requests earlier deletion where permitted.

logs.label: Security & audit logs
logs.body: Authentication, staff audit, and security event logs are retained for 12–24 months depending on severity and regulatory need.

marketing.label: Marketing & analytics
marketing.body: Optional analytics identifiers and marketing consents are retained until you withdraw consent or for 13 months of inactivity, whichever is sooner.

retention.deletion: When retention periods expire, we delete or irreversibly anonymise data where technically possible. Backups may persist for a limited additional cycle before automatic purging.

security.title: Technical & organisational security
security.intro: We implement measures designed to protect personal data against unauthorised access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction, including:

items.encryption: TLS encryption in transit for all web and API traffic; encryption at rest provided by our cloud infrastructure provider.
items.rls: PostgreSQL Row Level Security (RLS) so users can access only rows permitted by their role and ownership.
items.rbac: Role-based access control across individual, entity, staff, and system portals with middleware route guards.
items.sessions: Hashed session tokens, optional multi-device session review, and automatic expiry of inactive sessions.
items.monitoring: Logging of security-relevant events, bounce handling for email deliverability, and staff audit trails for administrative actions.

security.disclaimer: No method of transmission or storage is completely secure. If you believe your account has been compromised, change your password immediately and contact us.

amendments.title: Changes to this policy
amendments.p1: We may update this Privacy Policy to reflect legal, technical, or operational changes. When we make material changes, we will update the version number and effective date at the top of this page and, where appropriate, notify you by email or in-app notice.
amendments.p2: Continued use of JID after the effective date of an updated policy constitutes acceptance of the revised terms, unless applicable law requires explicit consent for specific processing activities.

contact.title: Contact us
contact.body: For privacy questions, data-subject requests, or to report a concern, contact us through the <contactLink>Contact</contactLink> page or the email address published there. Please include enough detail for us to verify your identity and locate your account.
contact.response: We aim to acknowledge requests within 10 business days and respond substantively within the timeframes required by applicable law.

meta.title: Terms of Service — JID
meta.description: Rules and conditions for using the JID employment and mentorship platform.

termsPage.title: Terms of Service
termsPage.summary: These Terms govern your access to and use of JID. Please read them carefully before creating an account or using any platform feature.

intro.p1: Welcome to JID (جِد). These Terms of Service ("Terms") form a binding agreement between you and the operator of the JID platform regarding your use of our websites, applications, and related services.
intro.p2: If you do not agree to these Terms, you must not access or use the platform. If you are using JID on behalf of a company or university, you represent that you have authority to bind that organisation.

acceptance.title: Acceptance of terms
acceptance.p1: You accept these Terms when you tick the acceptance box during registration, click "I agree", or otherwise continue using the platform after we publish an updated version.
acceptance.p2: Your use of JID is also subject to our <privacyLink>Privacy Policy</privacyLink>, which explains how we process personal data. In the event of a direct conflict between these Terms and the Privacy Policy regarding data processing, the Privacy Policy prevails for that subject matter.

responsibilities.title: User responsibilities
responsibilities.intro: You are responsible for your account and for all activity that occurs under it. You agree to:

items.accuracy: Provide accurate, current, and complete information in your profile, applications, and entity claims.
items.credentials: Keep your login credentials confidential and notify us promptly of any unauthorised use.
items.lawful: Use the platform only for lawful purposes and in compliance with applicable Saudi regulations and professional standards.
items.entity: Ensure that any company or university claim is submitted only by an authorised representative with documentary evidence when requested.
items.updates: Review notifications and update application statuses honestly so other users can rely on the platform's transparency features.

conduct.title: Platform conduct rules
conduct.intro: JID is built on mutual respect and transparency between candidates, mentors, and employers. The following rules apply to all users:

selfDeclaration.title: Self-declaration & anti-ghosting
selfDeclaration.p1: When you click to apply for an external job listing, JID may ask you to self-declare that you have submitted an application through the employer's own channel. This declaration creates an application record on your Radar so you and the platform can track progress.
selfDeclaration.p2: You must not falsely declare applications you did not submit, nor leave declared applications in a misleading state. Employers and candidates alike are expected to update statuses in good faith — ghosting (ignoring declared applications without status updates) undermines trust and may result in account restrictions.

items.honest: Declare applications only after you have genuinely applied via the employer's official process.
items.noGhosting: Respond to application status changes and employer communications within a reasonable time where the platform provides those channels.
items.status: Keep your Radar statuses accurate: move applications through saved, pending, under review, shortlisted, rejected, invited, or withdrawn as appropriate.
items.withdraw: Withdraw applications you are no longer pursuing rather than leaving them indefinitely in an active state.

items.harassment: Harassment, discrimination, threats, or abusive language toward any user.
items.spam: Spam, unsolicited bulk messaging, or fake listings designed to harvest personal data.
items.misrepresentation: Impersonation, falsified credentials, or misrepresentation of affiliation with any entity.
items.scraping: Automated scraping, reverse engineering, or circumvention of access controls without written permission.
items.malware: Uploading malware, attempting unauthorised access, or interfering with platform availability.

ip.title: Intellectual property
ip.platform: The JID name, logo, interface design, software, documentation, and original content we provide are owned by us or our licensors and are protected by applicable intellectual property laws. We grant you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable licence to use the platform for its intended purpose while your account is in good standing.
ip.userContent: You retain ownership of content you submit (profiles, CVs, job descriptions, mentor bios). By submitting content, you grant JID a worldwide, royalty-free licence to host, display, and process that content solely to operate and improve the service, including showing your public profile to users you authorise.
ip.restrictions: You may not copy, modify, distribute, or create derivative works from the platform or our branding except as expressly permitted in writing.

liability.title: Limitation of liability
liability.p1: JID is a technology platform that facilitates connections between users. We do not employ candidates, guarantee interviews or offers, or verify every statement made by users unless explicitly stated for a verified badge or approved claim.
liability.p2: To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia:

items.asIs: The platform is provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of uninterrupted or error-free operation.
items.thirdParty: We are not responsible for third-party websites, employer application systems, or content linked from job listings.
items.hiring: Hiring decisions, mentorship outcomes, and disputes between users are solely between the parties involved.
items.cap: Our aggregate liability for any claim arising from these Terms or your use of JID shall not exceed the greater of SAR 500 or the fees you paid us in the twelve months preceding the claim (currently zero for free-tier users).

termination.title: Suspension & termination
termination.p1: You may close your account at any time through settings or by contacting us. We may suspend or terminate access immediately if we reasonably believe you have breached these Terms, pose a security risk, or are required to do so by law.

items.user: Your right to access the platform ceases immediately, subject to any data retention described in the Privacy Policy.
items.breach: We may retain records of violations and audit logs as evidence for dispute resolution or legal compliance.
items.suspend: Suspended users may see a dedicated notice explaining the restriction and, where applicable, how to appeal.
items.effect: Provisions that by their nature should survive termination (intellectual property, liability limits, governing law) remain in effect.

governingLaw.title: Governing law & disputes
governingLaw.p1: These Terms are governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, without regard to conflict-of-law principles. Any dispute arising from or relating to these Terms or the platform shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, unless mandatory consumer protections require otherwise.
governingLaw.p2: Before initiating formal proceedings, we encourage you to contact us through the <contactLink>Contact</contactLink> page so we can attempt to resolve the matter in good faith.

meta.title: Contact Us — JID
meta.description: Reach the JID team for support, privacy requests, partnerships, and feedback.

contactPage.title: Contact us
contactPage.intro: Questions about your account, a privacy request, or a partnership inquiry? Send us a message and we will respond as soon as we can.

email.title: Email
email.body: Use the form and we route your message to the right team. For data-subject requests, choose the Privacy category.

response.title: Response time
response.body: We aim to acknowledge messages within 2 business days. Complex privacy or claim issues may take longer.

privacy.title: Data protection
privacy.body: Messages are stored securely and reviewed only by authorised staff for the purpose of handling your request.

info.privacyLink: Read our <privacyLink>Privacy Policy</privacyLink> and <pdplLink>PDPL notice</pdplLink> for details on how we process personal data.

form.heading: Send a message
form.subheading: All fields are required unless noted. Rate limit: 3 messages per hour per connection.

form.messageHint: Minimum 20 characters. Please include relevant job IDs or account email if applicable.

alert.body: Enable the master public switch only after candidate and company traction thresholds are met. Section toggles can be staged independently, but a premature public launch undermines credibility.

cta.title: Build your Harvard-style CV
cta.description: Create a professional PDF résumé pre-filled from your profile — ready for job applications.

builder.title: CV builder
builder.createdBanner: We created a draft from your profile. Review each section and export when ready.
builder.formPlaceholderBody: This column will host the active section editor. Server data is loaded via TanStack Query.

hints.title: Smart hints
hints.header: Lead with your name and a professional email. Keep contact details current for recruiters.
hints.education: List degrees in reverse chronological order. Include honors or relevant coursework when they strengthen your profile.
hints.experience: Use action verbs and quantify impact where possible. Focus on outcomes, not just duties.
hints.skills: Group technical and soft skills clearly. Prioritize skills mentioned in target job descriptions.
hints.additional: Add certifications, languages, or volunteer work that differentiates you from other candidates.

header.linkAnchorHint: Shown as clickable anchor text in the PDF (e.g. “LinkedIn”), not the raw URL.
header.customLinkHint: Label appears as the clickable anchor text in your PDF.
header.saveError: Could not save — check your connection

education.emptyDescription: Add your degrees, certificates, and training. You can reorder entries by dragging.
education.reorderHint: Drag entries to change order in your CV.
education.courseworkHint: Comma-separated or short list — shown in the PDF education section.
education.saveError: Could not save — check your connection

experience.emptyDescription: Add internships, jobs, and volunteer roles. Drag to reorder how they appear on your CV.
experience.reorderHint: Drag entries to change order in your CV.
experience.achievementsHint: Start each bullet with a strong action verb (e.g. Built, Led, Reduced). Focus on impact and measurable results.
experience.saveError: Could not save — check your connection

skills.technicalSkillsHint: Add tools, languages, and frameworks — shown as a comma-separated list in your PDF.
skills.languagesHint: Add languages with proficiency level — shown in the Skills section of your PDF.
skills.saveError: Could not save — check your connection

additional.groupedHint: Items are grouped by category in your PDF, matching the Additional section.
additional.title: Title
additional.saveError: Could not save — check your connection

disabled.missing_full_name: Add your full name to export
disabled.missing_education: Add at least one education entry
disabled.missing_experience: Add at least one experience entry
